rationale: The citing paper uses Thompson, Hamilton, and Rust (2005) in a specific theoretical role, not just as a list citation. It cites the feature-fatigue finding (pre-use overweighting of capability vs. post-use usability) to motivate why consumers may mispredict and under-realize value in later use, especially in the Study 4 setup on expected versus actual use. Although this cited work is not the central foundation of the full article, it meaningfully informs the paper's conceptual framing of pre- versus post-use mismatch.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's feature-fatigue insight as supporting evidence that pre-use evaluations can diverge from post-use outcomes. It leverages this idea to frame its own analysis of expectation-use miscalibration for really new versus incrementally new products.
